# Striatum Workflow Router

You are driving a Striatum workflow. Striatum is a local-first
runner whose authoritative live state lives in a daemon-owned
PostgreSQL instance under a `repository_id` scope per target
repository (D094 / RFC 0043). `.striatum/` next to the target repo
is operational scratch only. The runner — not your prose, and not
direct database access — advances state.

## When to invoke

- The operator asked to drive a workflow against a target repo.
- The operator mentioned "Striatum", `striatum`, "claim a packet",
  or a job/run/lease/work-packet vocabulary.
- A workflow file (typically `workflow.json`) is on disk.

## Authoritative reference

Call daemon MCP `tools/list` to see the live workflow-control methods
available to your token. Each work packet's `commands` block contains exact
CLI compatibility fallbacks for `ack`, `heartbeat`, `publish-artifact`,
`block`, `verdict` / `submit-review`, and `complete`; use those literally
only when MCP is unavailable or the operator explicitly requests CLI fallback.

## Where to look next

- **`striatum-scaffold`** — initialize a new workflow / start a run.
- **`striatum-claim-loop`** — the actual MCP work loop
  (session.register → work.await_packet → work.ack → artifact.publish →
  complete).
- **`striatum-supervise`** — long-lived supervised agent sessions.
- **`striatum-recover`** — `status`, `why`, `doctor`, recovery
  flows.

## What not to do

- Do not bypass the daemon (the CLI is the only client; the daemon is the single writer per D094 / RFC 0043); never open Postgres directly, and do not treat `.striatum/` scratch files as workflow state.
- Do not treat marker files, tmux panes, or terminal output as workflow state.
- Do not advance state by printing phrases; advance by calling the CLI verbs in your work packet.
- Do not capture stdout/stderr transcripts; D028 keeps them off by default.
- Do not derive bylines from job titles; use the byline supplied in your work packet.
- Do not parse a supervisor's own output for workflow state; supervisors send DEVNULL.
